heading: Maintenance and inspection of records
label: 1868 Maintenance and inspection of records
text: After the master jury wheel is emptied and refilled pursuant to , and after all persons selected to serve as jurors before the master wheel was emptied have completed such service, all records and papers compiled and maintained by the jury commission or clerk before the master wheel was emptied shall be preserved in the custody of the clerk for four years or for such longer period as may be ordered by a court, and shall be available for public inspection for the purpose of determining the validity of the selection of any jury.section 1863(b)(4) of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 416, 417 (, §§ 279, 280, , , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 23136 Stat. 1165Jan. 31, 1929, ch. 12645 Stat. 1145Section consolidates parts of sections 416, 417 of title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., with necessary changes in phraseology.The remaining portion of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., constitutes .section 416 of title 28section 1867 of this titleThe remainder of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., is incorporated in .section 417 of title 28section 1866 of this titleWords, “in the opinion of the court, disqualified” were substituted for “not an indifferent person, or is interested in the event of the cause”.

Amendments1968— substituted provisions for the maintenance and inspection of records in the hands of the commission or clerk before the master wheel was emptied for provisions covering the disqualification of the United States marshal or his deputy and the appointment of a disinterested person by the court.Pub. L. 90–274

Effective Date of 1968 AmendmentAmendment by effective 270 days after , except as to cases in which an indictment has been returned or a petit jury empaneled prior to such effective date, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 90–274section 104 of Pub. L. 90–274section 1861 of this titleMar. 27, 1968
